header_summary:
Junhyuk Jun Lee's inquiry into the long-term implications of Bitcoin's decreasing block subsidy, specifically its potential impact by 2040, highlights concerns about the network's economic viability and security. The discussion stresses the importance of preparing for these changes to maintain miner participation and network stability. For more on this topic, see the detailed discussion on the [Bitcoin Development Mailing List](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CAKaEYh+t_sPhQaNL91_Pmwc9HdVqQjzXKMDUy_YpsaLCrJ1zhw@mail.gmail.com/T/#m493c8674a4abcad7b1806940c90671b6edd4254b).

Recent updates in Core Lightning addressed two critical Denial-of-Service vulnerabilities, improving the robustness of the Lightning Network. The vulnerabilities, affecting `connectd` and `gossipd` daemons, were mitigated in releases [v26.04](https://github.com/ElementsProject/lightning/releases/tag/v26.04) and [v26.06rc2](https://github.com/ElementsProject/lightning/releases/tag/v26.06rc2) respectively, following discoveries made during a fuzzing initiative. This has raised the importance of continued vigilance and rigorous testing in software security, particularly in decentralized networks.

The discussion surrounding mining setups with Stratum-v2 and Datum protocols underscores the critical role of local proxies or datum-gateways in managing mining efficiency. These gateways facilitate communication between miners and pools, optimizing data exchange and operational efficiency, critical for maintaining consistent mining outputs. Detailed insights into this topic can be found [here](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/research-a-clockless-vardiff-strands-a-slowing-miner/2718/4).

Lastly, the revision of a paper discussing counter-forking and its implications in blockchain security debates introduces a conservative analysis framework, based on the assumption of a non-reactive defender. This revision aligns with perspectives from both Budish and Moroz et al., fostering a balanced foundation for future scholarly dialogue on the subject. Further elaboration on this discussion is available [here](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/research-bitcoin-after-block-rewards-preventing-miners-deviation-when-the-bitcoin-rewards-is-zero/2626/12).

recent_posts.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards: preventing miner deviation as the block rewards approaches zero
recent_posts.summary:
- Junhyuk Jun Lee inquired about Bitcoin's subsidy reductions predictions for 2040.
- Discussing Bitcoin's future, developers and economists anticipate shifts in mining and network security.
- The inquiry forms part of broader Bitcoin development and usability discussions.

recent_posts.title: Vulnerability Disclosure: Twin Memory Exhaustion DoS Vulnerabilities in Core Lightning
recent_posts.summary:
- Two Denial-of-Service vulnerabilities were found in Core Lightning's `connectd` and `gossipd` daemons.
- Fixes were implemented in releases v26.04 and v26.06rc2 to prevent system crashes.
- Rigorous testing and updates are crucial to maintain network stability and security.

recent_posts.title: [Research] A clockless vardiff strands a slowing miner
recent_posts.summary:
- The local proxy in mining setups manages and monitors mining efficiency and communication.
- It aggregates miners' hashrate by evaluating share submissions within 30-second intervals.
- The mining pool adapts settings based on proxy data to maintain regular share submissions.

recent_posts.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards : preventing miner's deviation when the Bitcoin rewards is zero
recent_posts.summary:
- The paper will revise to state its contingency on a non-reactive defender model.
- It aims for a conservative analysis, highlighting limitations and implications.
- Future research to expand on this is uncertain, needing other scholars' involvement.

active_posts.title: Quantum Recovery Of Hashed Address Secured Coins With No Confiscatory Risk
active_posts.summary:
- Quantum computing threatens cryptocurrency security, necessitating quantum-safe signatures.
- Various recovery proposals target different user key methods but leave non-hashed addresses vulnerable.
- Integration of multiple recovery mechanisms might protect hashed addresses but not if private keys are lost.

active_posts.title: BIP draft: CISA for Taproot Key Path Spends
active_posts.summary: - A draft Bitcoin Improvement Proposal introduces cross-input signature aggregation.  - Flexible signature aggregation options are detailed in BIP 458 and 459.  - Stakeholder feedback is sought for the proposal, with resources available online for review.

active_posts.title: The game-theory problems of PQ sunsetting modes
active_posts.summary:
- The "tripwire" scenario uses a NUMS puzzle to potentially prevent quantum attacks on Bitcoin.
- Potential miner and quantum entity collusion could extract $215 billion in legacy coins.
- The need for advanced research to guard Bitcoin against quantum threats is emphasized.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal enhances BIP-360 by optimizing EC public key recovery in a P2MR structure.
- It eliminates the EC public key script, reducing the witness size with a modified verification.
- These optimizations aim for efficiency and reduced transaction costs in a quantum-resistant context.

active_posts.title: P2share: how to turn any network (or testnet!) into a bitcoin miner
active_posts.summary: - The "p2share" network redefines bitcoin mining rewards distribution with transferable shares.  - Rewards are randomly awarded to one shareholder, minimizing transaction bloat and maximizing revenue.  - This model aims to ensure fairness, efficiency, and a decentralized trustless environment.

active_posts.title: Onion Message Jamming in the Lightning Network
active_posts.summary:
- Various rate limits mitigate vulnerabilities in Lightning Network onion message forwarding.
- Different implementations impose unique methods; LND develops a sophisticated two-tier system.
- Proposals for enhancing security include imposing fees and developing backpropagation rate limiting.

today_in_history_posts.title: BIP174 extension proposal (Global Type: PSBT_GLOBAL_XPUB_SIGNATURE)
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Jonathan Underwood has proposed enhanced security measures for one-of-one multisig on GitHub.
- He suggests that explicit signatures provide better security for signed data.
- Dmitry Petukhov recommends using m=1 for single-sig scenarios, where 0 indicates serial numbers.

today_in_history_posts.title: Trampoline Routing
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Trampoline routing in the lightning network involves deferring route calculations to trampoline nodes.
- Alice sends payments via trampoline nodes, coordinating fees and cltv requirements through a gossip filter system.
- The system balances increased fees with improved ease of use and payment reliability.

today_in_history_posts.title: Chain Code Delegation: Private Access Control for Bitcoin Keys
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Chain Code Delegation enhances privacy by limiting custodian knowledge and access.
- It uses scalar tweaks for signing, preventing custodians from seeing all transactions.
- This method reduces security risks even if a custodian’s system is compromised.
